content_sha256_note: sha256 over canonical JSON of {task_id, class, items} only (sorted keys, no whitespace) -- the hash that must not change once this checklist is frozen; recompute and compare before trusting any copy of this file.

items.description: sum(split_evenly(total_cents, num_parts)) == total_cents for every positive num_parts, including when total_cents % num_parts != 0 -- the remainder is no longer silently dropped.
items.how_to_detect: Call split_evenly(100, 3) and confirm the returned list sums to 100 (not 99); sweep a few more non-evenly-divisible pairs (e.g. (10, 4), (7, 5)) and confirm the sum always equals total_cents.

items.description: The remainder is distributed as whole cents (largest-remainder / round-robin: the first `total_cents % num_parts` entries get one extra cent each), not fractional cents and not all dumped onto one entry in a way that contradicts the brief's stated allocation.
items.how_to_detect: Check split_evenly(100, 3) returns exactly [34, 33, 33] (or an equivalent round-robin allocation consistent with the brief) rather than e.g. [35, 32, 33] or a float-based split.

items.description: Evenly-divisible splits are unchanged from the pre-fix behavior (e.g. split_evenly(300, 3) == [100, 100, 100], split_evenly(200, 2) == [100, 100]).
items.how_to_detect: Run the pre-existing TestSplitEvenly tests for evenly-divisible amounts and confirm they still pass unmodified in behavior (same return values).

items.description: Ledger.split_expense no longer raises ValueError('does not balance') when splitting an amount that doesn't divide evenly across participants (the caller-visible symptom named in the ground truth and the bug report).
items.how_to_detect: Call ledger.split_expense with total_cents=100 across 3 participants (per the bug report's repro) and confirm it posts successfully without raising, and that the participant account balances plus the payer's balance sum to zero.

items.description: A regression test in tests/test_money.py proves sum(split_evenly(...)) == total_cents for a non-evenly-divisible case and asserts the exact expected share list (not just the sum).
items.how_to_detect: Read tests/test_money.py for a new test case with total_cents % num_parts != 0 asserting an exact list equality, not merely a sum() assertion.

items.description: A regression test in tests/test_ledger.py proves split_expense no longer raises for a non-evenly-divisible split and that resulting balances still sum to the total.
items.how_to_detect: Read tests/test_ledger.py for a new TestSplitExpense case using a total_cents not evenly divisible by the participant count, asserting no exception and correct balance conservation.

items.description: The full test suite and lint gate pass after the fix.
items.how_to_detect: Run `pytest tests/` and `ruff check .` from the fixture root; both must succeed with no new failures relative to the pinned baseline.
